title: India, China neighbours that can't be parted: Beijing envoy
content: China's envoy to India, Xu Feihong, asserted that India and China's progress stems from their people's efforts, not external aid, subtly countering US remarks. He urged win-win cooperation and cautioned against those seeking to sow discord between the neighbours. Xu also highlighted the need for China and India to champion developing nations amidst rising global challenges.

title: Nirav Modi defiant, blames CBI and ED for destroying his businesses
content: Appearing defiant in London, Nirav Modi testified in a civil trial, denying owing Bank of India over $10.7 million. He claimed banks, CBI, and ED destroyed his 20-year business, asserting no fraud against Punjab National Bank. Modi alleged PNB colluded with CBI, leading to an FIR without his input, and ED's actions halted his operations.

title: India, Dhaka should address sensitive issues with sincerity, candour: Envoy
content: Bangladesh High Commissioner Riaz Hamidullah emphasized sincere dialogue on trade, security, and resource sharing for a prosperous future built on shared democratic beliefs. He highlighted the need for mutual respect and interdependence, recalling India's crucial support during Bangladesh's 1971 Liberation War and the spirit of equality and justice.

title: CEO of America's largest bank reveals one work-from-home trend he didn't notice earlier
content: JP Morgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticizes remote work, citing a lack of attentiveness in virtual meetings and its detrimental impact on young employees' learning and emotional intelligence. He argues that in-office presence is crucial for skill development and project ownership, contrasting it with the 'game playing' and reduced accountability he observes in remote setups.

title: No shortage of oil and gas, won't impose curbs: Govt
content: The government has ruled out restrictions on petrol and diesel sales, assuring citizens of ample oil and gas availability and robust import arrangements.  Despite global fuel use curbs and misinformation-driven queues, India's petroleum and LPG supply remains secure and under control, with reserves and ongoing procurements ensuring uninterrupted dispensing at all retail outlets.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 8: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Dhurandhar 1' record of Rs 840
content: As Aditya Dhar’s espionage masterpiece Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge enters its eighth day on Thursday, March 26, 2026, the Ranveer Singh starrer is rewriting the history of Indian cinema. Having already shattered the Rs. 1,000 crore worldwide milestone in record time, the film is now tracking at nearly double the efficiency of previous blockbusters like 'Jawan', 'Pathaan', and 'Animal.' Despite its sprawling 3-hour and 49-minute runtime, the sequel's domestic net collection has surged to more than Rs. 630 crore, fueled by unprecedented demand for midnight screenings and a gripping narrative that sees Jaskirat Singh Rangi, played by Ranveer Singh deeper than ever into the Karachi underworld as Hamza Ali Mazari.

title: Fewer flights, no fare caps: Domestic airfares set to soar
content: Domestic air travel is poised for a significant price increase this summer, mirroring international flight costs. A 12% reduction in weekly domestic flights, coupled with the removal of fare caps and anticipated Aviation Turbine Fuel hikes, will drive up ticket prices. Only financially robust airlines are expected to maintain operations as demand and operating costs fluctuate.

title: Get rid of old assumptions: Microsoft HR makes it clear to employees that it's time to change
content: Microsoft's Chief People Officer Amy Coleman announced a significant HR restructuring, prioritizing adaptability and speed for the AI era. This shakeup includes leadership changes and new teams focused on accelerating HR operations, employee experience, and workforce skilling. The move signals a shift towards agility and a faster pace for the tech giant.

title: PM Modi to meet CMs today on West Asia conflict preparedness
content: Prime Minister Modi is set to convene a video conference with Chief Ministers, excluding those from poll-bound states, to assess national readiness for potential disruptions stemming from the West Asia conflict. The "Team India" spirit will guide discussions on energy supply concerns and preparedness, with the cabinet secretariat to engage excluded states separately.

title: Delhi leads state-wise donations to national parties, shows ADR analysis
content: Delhi leads national parties in donations for FY 2024-25, contributing Rs 2,639.4 crore. Maharashtra and Gujarat follow with significant contributions. The Association for Democratic Reforms report highlights a substantial increase in total donations, with corporate entities being the primary source.

title: Dowry claims lives of thousands, do not give bail for such deaths mechanically: SC
content: The Supreme Court has cautioned high courts against granting bail in dowry death cases, deeming a Patna High Court order unsustainable. The bench emphasized the gravity of dowry deaths, a social evil causing thousands of women's unnatural deaths, and directed the accused husband to surrender.

title: Social media addiction trial: Meta and Google may be facing their 'Tobacco Moment'
content: Two recent jury verdicts have placed social media companies like Meta and Google on notice, suggesting potential product liability for harm to minors. A New Mexico jury awarded $375 million against Meta for exposing minors to harmful content, while a California jury awarded $6 million against Meta and YouTube for contributing to a young woman's mental health struggles.

title: Paul Skenes pulled early after Pittsburgh Pirates opening day collapse
content: Paul Skenes had a shocking start to the 2026 MLB season, lasting just two outs and giving up five runs. Fielding errors and control issues made things worse, leading to an early exit. Manager Don Kelly said it was a pitch count decision. Despite strong preseason confidence, Skenes now faces an early setback but no injury concerns.

title: President urged not to grant assent to transgender bill
content: Over 140 legal professionals and activists have urged President Murmu to reject the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026. They argue the bill, passed hastily, violates constitutional rights by removing the right to self-perceived gender identity and introducing a medical board for identity certificates, potentially excluding marginalized transgender individuals from essential protections.

title: You've got mail: J&K's first postwoman clocks 25 letters a day, 30 years on foot
content: In Shopian's Hirapora village, 55-year-old Ulfat Bano has been Kashmir's first postwoman for over three decades, delivering mail on foot through challenging terrain and harsh winters. Despite the physical toll, her passion for connecting people fuels her dedication.  She finds immense satisfaction in bringing good news and parcels to her community, viewing her work as a vital service.

title: A’ja Wilson recounts meeting role model Beyoncé at Formula 1
content: A’ja Wilson reflected on meeting Beyoncé at a Formula 1 event, calling it a moment where “Nothing else matters.” The Las Vegas Aces star continues to grow her influence beyond basketball, blending fashion with elite performance. While embracing style and major platforms like the Met Gala, Wilson remains focused on her game, balancing off-court presence with her dominance and achievements on the court.

title: Megan Thee Stallion opens up on depression amid Klay Thompson romance
content: Megan Thee Stallion, the six time Grammy winner, has turned heads with her high profile relationship with the Dallas Mavericks’ star player, Klay Thompson. As fans root for an engagement announcement, Megan Thee Stallion's heartbreaking statements about feeling "too much" have sparked chaos and made fans emotional over the pain she has gone through in her life before she met the NBA legend.

title: Candace Owens questions Erika Kirk’s location during Charlie Kirk attack
content: Candace Owens has intensified scrutiny over Erika Kirk’s location during the assassination of Charlie Kirk. She claims Erika was inside a secretive, unmarked aviation building, contradicting her account of being at a medical facility. Owens also pointed to a lack of witnesses, calling the situation “secretive,” and raising broader questions about the timeline and transparency surrounding the events of that day.

title: Tectone YouTube ban leads to Rumble switch, streamer quits Twitch and YouTube
content: Tectone has announced a significant move, leaving both YouTube and Twitch to become an exclusive streamer on Rumble. This decision follows a recent one-week ban from YouTube for "excessive swearing," prompting him to seek a platform with more perceived freedom. His first Rumble stream is scheduled for March 28, though censored videos will still appear on YouTube.

title: Vertical peril: How faulty elevators are claiming lives in India’s high-rises
content: Recent lift accidents across India, including tragic fatalities in Kolkata and Hyderabad, highlight critical safety lapses. Mechanical failures, negligence in maintenance, and dangerous improvisations have led to devastating consequences. These incidents underscore the urgent need for rigorous inspections, professional repairs, and enhanced safety protocols to prevent further loss of life.

title: Watch: Shami's latest act triggers big reaction from Langer
content: Mohammed Shami is training hard ahead of IPL 2026, aiming to return to India’s squad. The 35-year-old impressed LSG coach Justin Langer with his yorkers, who called them “breathtaking.” Langer also praised Shami’s leadership among younger players. With selectors overlooking him, the IPL offers Shami a crucial chance to prove himself and push for a comeback.

title: Will Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce make their red carpet debut at iHeartRadio Music Awards?
content: Taylor Swift's special in-person appearance at the 2026 #iHeartAwards has fans buzzing, especially with her leading nominations. The biggest question is whether Travis Kelce will join her on the red carpet for a coordinated debut, fueling wedding rumors. This marks Swift's first major awards show of the year, hinting at significant career or personal news.

title: Freebies over development? K'taka cuts programs to fund welfare schemes: CAG report
content: Karnataka's finances are strained by welfare schemes, forcing cuts in other programs. The CAG report reveals Rs 52,525 crore spent on five guarantee schemes, consuming a significant portion of revenue and necessitating increased borrowing. This fiscal pressure impacts capital expenditure and raises concerns about future growth and debt servicing obligations.

title: What is ‘launching soon’? White House official page posts strange videos amid US‑Iran war
content: Amid escalating US‑Iran tensions, the White House posted two cryptic videos, one deleted within 90 minutes, that quickly fueled online speculation. Lacking context or narrative, the clips prompted theories ranging from apps and space missions to military strikes, with many users conjuring ominous conspiracies. Despite millions of views, the White House offered no clarification, leaving the posts mysterious and widely debated.

title: Pandya wins hearts with promise kept, rewards Wankhede groundstaff
content: Hardik Pandya fulfilled a promise to reward Wankhede groundstaff who supported his late-night World Cup training, earning praise for his humility. The two-time T20 World Cup winner acknowledged their behind-the-scenes efforts while continuing to shine on the field. Reflecting on life, he said, “Jab se Mahieka aayi hai, life mein bas jeet hi jeet hai.”

title: Kick streamer ShnaggyHose accuses Cheesur of harming girlfriend Kaelyn
content: ShnaggyHose has accused Cheesur of harming his girlfriend Kaelyn during a livestream, with the clip going viral online. The claims were made during a heated exchange, but no proof has been publicly confirmed. Cheesur has not responded so far. The situation remains unclear, and viewers are waiting for further updates or an official statement.

title: Arjun Rampal's girlfriend hits back at the article criticising 'Dhurandhar 2' actor
content: Arjun Rampal's closing remark of 'Bharat Mata Ki Jai' at an awards show, following his role in 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' and recalling the 26/11 attacks, sparked a debate. Shobhaa De criticized the statement as a display of loyalty and manipulation, while others defended Rampal's patriotism.

title: How to get Darkbringer Sword in Crimson Desert
content: Players can find the powerful Darkbringer Sword in Crimson Desert. This weapon is located in the northern Pailune region, within the White Wastes Sanctuary. The Darkbringer Sword features the unique Abyss Gear, Ator's Orb. This orb fires explosive projectiles during Forward Slashes, making the sword devastating. Players can also transfer Ator's Orb to other weapons for added flexibility.

title: 'Franchise has backed you': Irfan Pathan challenges LSG star to step up in IPL 2026
content: Irfan Pathan emphasizes IPL 2026 as a crucial season for Mayank Yadav, urging him to repay Lucknow Super Giants' faith after injuries hampered his progress.  LSG's strong pace attack, led by Mohammed Shami and supported by a talented Indian unit, is highlighted.  Pathan also notes LSG's ongoing search for a distinct franchise identity.

title: Centre takes big step to remove China hand in India's drones
content: The Ministry of Defence has released a draft framework to ensure a safe and secure ecosystem for small drones. Drones are changing the way wars are fought and India too is heavily investing in this emerging field. The drones will be deployed across India's vast frontiers and in varying terrains against enemies with advanced technologies. This framework would ensure that the drones operated in war function in a safe and effective manner.

title: Shubman Gill joins criticism of Impact Player rule, says ‘It takes the skill out’
content: Gujarat Titans captain Shubman Gill has voiced concerns about the IPL Impact Player rule. He believes it reduces the skill element in cricket. Several other Indian players share similar sentiments. The rule allows an extra player, impacting the traditional balance of the game. Gill feels it makes matches more predictable. The rule is set to continue until 2027.

title: Mines, missiles, & mountains: Why reopening Strait of Hormuz is no easy task
content: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil route, is currently blocked by Iran. This action follows a US-Israel offensive and the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Iran has created a tense maritime chokehold, impacting global oil prices. While diplomatic talks are ongoing, reopening the strait presents significant geographical and military challenges.

title: Who is Ben Azelart? YouTuber claims $1.2m earnings from one video
content: Ben Azelart is trending after claiming he earned $1.2 million from a single YouTube video on Sidemen Inside Season 3. The clip, shared by Jack Manifold on X, quickly went viral. Despite this, Azelart says his success came from steady growth, not one viral moment, as he built his career from skateboarding videos to global content creation.

title: IPL 2026: Another setback for RCB as star pacer ruled out after Josh Hazlewood
content: Sri Lankan pacer Nuwan Thushara will miss the Indian Premier League. Sri Lanka Cricket denied him a No Objection Certificate. This follows a new strict fitness policy. Players must meet new physical benchmarks for overseas leagues. Thushara reportedly fell short of these standards. This could cost him his contract with Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

title: 'Keep ‌blowing them away': Trump renews warning as he claims Iran 'begging' for deal
content: President Trump stated Iran is "begging" for a deal to end the war and warned of further strikes if nuclear ambitions are not abandoned. He indicated diplomacy might be closing, while US military operations are ahead of schedule.  The conflict has resulted in over 3,000 deaths and millions displaced.

title: US-Iran war impact: India’s crude imports from Russia near all-time high
content: The inflows of Russian crude oil have risen sharply since the US-Iran war began and imports via the Strait of Hormuz were disrupted. In fact, crude imports from Russia are now nearing lifetime monthly highs!

title: PM Modi to attend G7 summit in France from June 15-17, focus on global economy & Hormuz
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to attend the G7 summit in France from June 15-17. This confirmation came during high-level talks where India's crucial role in addressing global economic issues and fostering solidarity was highlighted. Discussions also centered on the Middle East, with both nations agreeing to coordinate on securing the vital Strai of Hormuz.

title: Haryana’s cotton farms holding on by a thread
content: The Bt cotton revolution of the early 2000s had farmers in Haryana’s Hisar, Sirsa, Fatehabad, and Jind reaping rewards. Now, the pink bollworm is slashing yields and squeezing profits and the MSP is not enough. As farmers ditch cotton for paddy, the impact is soon likely to be felt by farm labourers, traders, and the economy as a whole, reports Ashok Kumar

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: Bitter battle for nine seats that can turn electoral fortunes
content: The LDF, the UDF and the NDA are locked in three-cornered fights in the constituencies of Nemom, Kazhakkoottam, Vattiyoorkkavu, Aranmula, Pala, Thrissur, Nattika, Palakkad and Manjeshwaram. The outcomes from these seats could even determine the final outcome of the polls.

title: Tamil Nadu’s Nitish Kumar or assertive chief? Edappadi Palaniswami confronts a battle of perceptions
content: DMK claims AIADMK is subservient to the Modi government, framing the Assembly election as Tamil Nadu vs Delhi; BJP leaders avoid naming Mr. Palaniswami as NDA’s CM face, but he asserts himself in seat-sharing announcements

title: President urged not to grant assent to Transgender Amendment Bill
content: The letter was written by All-India Feminist Alliance (ALIFA), a pan-India collective of grassroots organisations, along with National Alliance for Justice, Accountability and Rights (NAJAR); the groups said they were ‘extremely alarmed and distressed at the undue and unjustifiable haste’ with which the Bill was passed in Parliament

title: Parliamentary panel pitches for RPF-like force for highways
content: The parliamentary committee said a patrol service could improve real-time response on high-accident corridors and expressways during the ‘golden hour’ enforce speed discipline on access-controlled roads, and deter damage to highway infrastructure

title: India has oil for 60 days, LPG for a month: Centre
content: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said all petrol pumps across the country are adequately stocked and operating normally, with no rationing of petrol or diesel

title: Court rejects Ramadoss’ plea on PMK’s mango symbol
content: The court makes it clear that all observations are limited to deciding the interim applications and wwill not affect the final outcome of the suit.

title: NHRC issues notice to Chhattisgarh government over death of 285 inmates in different jails of State in four years
content: State government in the Assembly cited suicide and chronic ailments as the reasons behind the inmates’ death

title: Bihar will remain indebted to Nitish Kumar for building a ‘New Bihar’: Nitin Nabin
content: Mr. Nabin, who is also going to Rajya Sabha along with Mr. Kumar announced that it might be his final official government engagement as an MLA

title: Bihar’s youth now making their mark in technology field: IT Minister Shreyasi Singh
content: IIT Patna Incubation Centre signed two MOUs with AIIMS Patna & JHPIEGO. IT Minister addressed 10th Foundation Day celebration of IIT Patna Incubation Centre

title: Ro-ro ferries fitted with Automatic Identification System
content: The AIS system enables the ferry master to monitor the movement of nearby vessels and crafts, thereby enhancing navigational safety

title: Liquor no more be served near Shree Jagannath Temple, Puri
content: Odisha’s new excise policy imposes 0.5% de-addiction Cess and prohibits opening of new ‘ON’ shops in rural areas

title: Woman techie killed as 18-year-old boy runs her over by car over society dispute in Indore
content: A CCTV footage of the incident has surfaced on the internet showing a white car charging on the crowd

title: The surging cost of health care in Kerala
content: The interest shown by global private equity firms in Kerala’s private health sector has given rise to apprehensions that medical treatment may become a costly affair. A.S. Jayanth finds out A look at the possible fallout of the massive foreign investments in Kerala's hospitals

title: Marumagal Vaaku: an allegorical Tamil short story on the emotional coercion of voters
content: Unlike high-pitched electioneering the State is known for, the TVK’s campaign seems in part to be unfolding quietly within households. What unfolds is reminiscent of the short story Marumagal Vaaku (Dauther-in-law’s Vote) by the late Tamil writer Krishnan Nambi

title: Minor detained for stabbing woman and her brother to death in Bengaluru
content: A 16-year-old boy allegedly stabbed a woman and her brother to death following a dispute linked to an alleged extramarital relationship in northwest Bengaluru

title: Eshwar Khandre says Karnataka govt. will recover money from IAF for giving 58 acres of forest land to BMRCL for compensation
content: Forest Minister B. Eshwar Khandre on Thursday told the Legislative Assembly that the Indian Air Force (IAF) authorities were not only in ‘illegal possession’ of forest land in Jarakbande Kaval village of Bengaluru North taluk as the land granted to them had been cancelled, but it had also given about 58 acres of forest land to BMRCL for monetary compensation

title: PDP asks ruling NC to clear stand on Bill proposing Chenab Valley, Pir Panjal as new divisions in J&K
content: The PDP seeks new administrative divisions, including a Chenab Division with headquarters at Doda and a Pir Panjal Division with headquarters at Rajouri, in addition to the existing Jammu and Kashmir divisions

title: East, Central corporations to present their first annual budgets on March 27
content: The remaining three corporations in the Greater Bengaluru Authority area will present their budgets on March 28

title: In a first, multi-layered security and holding areas introduced at Bengaluru’s M. Chinnaswamy Stadium
content: On Thursday, the State Expert Committee overseeing the overhaul of the stadium conducted a final drill and formally announced that the venue has undergone all upgrades satisfactorily

title: Jaishankar, French counterpart discuss Iran, Hormuz at G7 meet
content: The participation of External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar in the G7 meetings signified “the importance France attaches to closely associating India — currently holding the BRICS presidency — with its G7 presidency”, the French government said

title: Threat to life, says Samajwadi Party Lok Sabha MP Afzal Ansari
content: He also said selective targeting of individuals takes place through movies and individuals facing numerous cases are patronized by the Uttar Pradesh government

title: AIADMK allotted Ministers’ constituencies to allies as it is colluding with DMK: Seeman
content: He alleges that this is to ensure the defeat of its allies, including BJP. Criticising the Congress, he accuses the party of having harmed the interests of the Tamil people while in power at the Centre

title: Kerala VHSE gets AB-Dual recognition
content: With this, the Vocational Higher Secondary Education wing of the General Education department will be able to design courses according to the National Skills Qualifications Framework (NSQF) and give them approval

title: Farmers dying by suicide in W.B. due to faulty policies of TMC, claims BJP president
content: W.B. BJP president Samik Bhattacharya cited names of farmers who have ended their lives in the past two months. The BJP leader said that the people will respond to the faulty policies of Trinamool Congress in the upcoming elections

title: ₹36.50 cr. authorised to clear victims’ compensation fund arrears, Kerala HC informed
content: This comes in the wake of the court observing recently that it would pass orders to attach the State government’s treasury accounts if the government failed to pay the amount within a week

title: Kerala assembly polls 2026: Though not in the fray, they are in the poll arena as campaigners
content: Some former legislators and leaders who are not in the fray this time  are still in the limelight as campigners

title: Annamalai unhappy with constituencies allocated by AIADMK
content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president is learnt to have requested the party leadership not to identify any seat for him as he has decided against contesting in the Assembly election. But he will  continue to work “as a party karyakarta for all BJP and NDA  candidates”

title: Zubeen Garg memorial in Assam casts poll shadow on politics
content: Visiting the site where the last rites of Zubeen Garg were performed has been a ritual for many Brahmaputra Valley candidates in the poll-bound State

title: Slovak detained along Mizoram-Myanmar border, likely to be deported
content: The man, a resident of Thailand, was allegedly trying to cross into Myanmar to meet a friend; he did not have a protected area permit; detention comes after American, Ukrainians tried to cross border to train armed ethnic groups

title: 10 killed, 35 injured as bus collides with mini-truck in M.P.’s Chhindwara
content: The deceased include drivers of both vehicles; the bus was returning from an event attended by Chief Minister Mohan Yadav

title: India, other friendly countries granted passage through Hormuz: Iran
content: Ships of India, China, Russia, Iraq, and Pak. can pass through the Strait, says Iran Foreign Minister, who thanks India, Sri Lanka for ‘significant help’; ‘sovereignty’ established over waterway, he adds

title: Assam election: NPP fields candidates in three constituencies for the April 9 election
content: The party led by Meghalaya Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma eyes a bigger goal of becoming a political force across eight Northeastern States by contesting three seats in Assam

title: Indian among two killed in UAE by debris of intercepted missiles
content: An Indian and a Pakistani were killed in Abu Dhabi by missile debris; the Indian Embassy offers condolences and support

title: Indian Navy launches ‘Operation Urja Suraksha’ to secure energy lifelines through Strait of Hormuz
content: Navy identifies all India-bound ships carrying LPG, LNG, crude oil for protected transit; warships provide escort, offer navigational support; four LPG carriers, one crude tanker escorted so far

title: Tunnel road is most viable solution to Bengaluru’s traffic congestion: D.K. Shivakumar
content: D.K. Shivakumar estimates that around ₹3,000 crore be will required for land acquisition and ₹17,000 crore for tunnel road construction

title: CBI arrests human trafficking ‘kingpin’ in Myanmar-based cyber scam compounds case
content: The accused has been identified as Sunil Nellathu Ramakrishnan, alias Krish, a resident of Mumbai. During the CBI probe, he emerged as one of the main “facilitators” in the trafficking network, through which victims were being illegally transported for exploitation in the scam centres in Southeast Asia.

title: Fuel crisis updates: PM Modi to interact with CMs; review preparedness, plans on West Asia conflict
content:
India has 60 days of oil stocks, one month of LPG supplies, says Government; 
Nayara Energy raised petrol prices by ₹5 per litre and diesel by ₹3 a litre

title: Assembly Elections updates: Illicit inducements worth over ₹400 crore seized in 4 States and UT in the last month, ECI says
content: Electronic Seizure Management System (ESMS) was activated on February 26; SDPI pulls out of Manjeswaram contest, easing vote-split concerns for UDF

title: Willing to work with India for ‘common interests’ in Global South, BRICS: China
content: Following a meeting between Foreign Minister Wang Yi and outgoing Indian ambassador Pradeep Kumar Rawat in Beijing, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian says the two sides view each other as “development opportunities”

title: Tonali, Kean goals send Italy to WC playoff final
content: Italy finally won a match in the World Cup playoffs. Now the four-time champion needs to win one more to avoid failing to qualify for a third straight time.

title: IU's Cignetti rips transfer Marsh for gold cleats
content: Indiana coach Curt Cignetti ripped transfer wide receiver Nick Marsh for wearing gold cleats on the first day of spring practice for the defending national champs.

title: Auriemma: Title IX 'pretty much out the window'
content: UConn women's basketball coach Geno Auriemma on Thursday said Title IX legislation, in practice, is "pretty much out the window."

title: McGonigle lives up to hype with 4 hits in debut
content: Rookie Kevin McGonigle hit the first pitch he saw for a bases-loaded, two-run double and became the youngest MLB player with four or more hits on Opening Day since 1990.

title: Boston College hires UConn assistant Murray
content: Boston College hired UConn assistant Luke Murray as its next head coach, the school announced Thursday.

title: Misiorowski, Brewers tie MLB record with 20 K's
content: Brewers starter Jacob Misiorowski and four relievers buried the White Sox in an avalanche of 20 strikeouts, the first time a team has recorded that many in nine innings on Opening Day.

title: Pirates pull shaky Skenes in 1st amid OF miscues
content: Paul Skenes recorded just two outs before being pulled from the Pirates' season opener, the shortest outing of his MLB career.

title: Wade rejoining LSU; NC State AD feels 'lied to'
content: Will Wade said in a statement posted to X on Thursday that he is leaving his job as the coach at NC State to return to LSU.

title: 🏈 NFL draft: Biggest needs for all 32 teams
content: Our NFL reporters listed the top three positional needs for all 32 teams ahead of April's draft, and our draft experts found non-Round 1 fits to fill them.

title: Updated NBA rookie rankings: Why the ROY race is all but over
content: Where do the NBA's top rookies stack up with a few weeks left in the regular season? We stacked the top 10.

title: Predicting the 2026 MLB season, from the playoffs and World Series to MVP and Cy Young
content: Can the Dodgers three-peat? Will Skubal and Skenes win another Cy Young? Our experts make their predictions for the 2026 baseball season.

title: 'It's all or nothing': UCLA veterans ready for final NCAA title run
content: Lauren Betts and her teammates have unfinished business since falling short at the Final Four a year ago.

title: Lunardi: Want Cinderella back? Set an NCAA tournament eligibility floor
content: The blame for Cinderella's demise is misplaced. Here's why we haven't seen her -- and how we can get her back.

title: Ranking coaches and teams that are likely to improve in 2026
content: Curt Cignetti reset how we view coaching turnarounds while Oklahoma State, with a new coach, has nowhere to go but up.

title: An early look at 2027 NFL free agency: Which playmakers could be available next March?
content: Which top players might be available in next year's NFL free agency? We picked out 49 stars to keep an eye on.

title: Ranking all 32 free agency classes: Which teams most improved their rosters?
content: How did every team fare in March, taking into account free agent signings, trades, extensions and departures?

title: How Arbeloa has won over doubters and reinvigorated Real Madrid's season
content: Whether it's the coach, the players, or both, Real Madrid have improved since Álvaro Arbeloa took over in January, and he could yet save their season.

title: The unexpected path that led Morgan and Frankie Price to Arkansas
content: The Price sisters have led the Arkansas gymnastics team in their final season of the sport -- and they hope it ends with an NCAA championship berth.

title: Draft guide: Rankings, tips, strategy, stats and more
content: All the rankings, projections, cheat sheets, strategy and analysis you need for the fantasy baseball season.

title: 'The KING ... Shot by ME': Caitlin Clark photographs LeBron James at Lakers-Pacers
content: The Indiana Fever star was behind the lens as a credentialed photographer at Wednesday night's Lakers-Pacers matchup.

title: Each MLB team's most interesting giveaway this season
content: Not all ballpark giveaways are created equal. Here's each team's most creative offering for the upcoming campaign.

title: Men's tournament: Three bets and two Eliminator picks for the round of 16
content: How to bet on top-2 seeds Purdue, Houston and Duke in Thursday's and Friday's regional semifinal games.

title: Women's tournament: UConn still title favorite as Virginia upset shakes up the odds
content: Odds for each team to advance through the bracket and to win the 2026 women's college basketball championship.

title: 'What I'm thinking is arena football but outdoors': How Mike Repole is remaking the UFL
content: Repole is the man behind many of the UFL's changes, including smaller venues and the four-point field goal.

title: 25 trends to know before draft season begins
content: Mike Clay shares his favorite notes from the research that informed his fantasy projections for 2026.

title: 'Definitely not my final chapter': A refreshed Israel Adesanya isn't done yet
content: He may have traded right hooks and calf kicks for DJ decks last year, but Israel Adesanya insists he is motivated to continue his UFC career -- starting this weekend against Joe Pyfer.

title: Max Verstappen vs. F1's new rules: Every complaint this season -- and our verdict
content: Amid a wave of complaints from F1's four-time world champion Max Verstappen, ESPN's Nate Saunders examines every one and gives his verdict on if they are warranted.

title: From IndyCar royalty to NASCAR dynasty: How Penske conquered American racing
content: Roger Penske's racing enterprises have long been synonymous with IndyCar, but now they've built the most decorated team in recent NASCAR history, too.

title: Cost of the World Cup: How much might a USMNT fan spend to follow their team?
content: From tickets to hotels, how expensive is this summer's big event? Let's find out -- through the eyes of a USMNT fan on the trip of a lifetime.

title: World Cup playoff preview, predictions: Who will book the final six spots?
